untranslatable_reason: Signal references a proxy index (DJTA) with custom volatility bands (±3% from MA) applied to a target market (S&P 500). The allowed indicator set does not include Bollinger Bands with custom width specifications or cross-market proxy logic. The signal requires: (1) bands calculated as MA ± 3% (not standard BB), (2) reference to an external index as entry trigger, and (3) confirmation of extreme deviations. Standard bb_upper/bb_lower use 2 standard deviations, not percentage offsets. Cannot accurately model the 'rare extreme deviations below lower band' condition without access to the proxy instrument's actual bands or custom band construction.
translation_notes: Attempted simplified translation using SMA_20 as the center reference with long entry above and short entry below, but this loses the critical volatility band logic and cross-market proxy mechanism that defines the strategy. The original rule's strength lies in detecting rare extreme moves in a correlated proxy—a concept that cannot be faithfully expressed with single-instrument indicators alone.
